sections.paragraph:
-

(1) Arabic معِطَ to be smooth naked, hence to be polished, sharp; see מָעֹט ; transp. مَعَطَ prob. to scrape, to scrape off (comp. معس to rub, also מָרַט, since ע and ר are kindred letters to each other), whence מְעַט prop. a scraping, scrap (ein Span, Spänchen), hence a little. Hence comes the denom.-

(2) to be little, few. Lev 25:16, לְפִי מְעֹט הַשָּׁים “according to the fewness of the years,” Exo 12:4 Neh 9:32 also to be made few, to be diminished, Psa 107:39 Isa 21:17 Pro 13:11.

Piel מִעֵט intrans. i.q. Kal, Ecc 12:3.

Hiphil, הִמְעִיט-

(1) to make few, to diminish, Levit. 25:16 Num 26:54 33:54 Jerem. 10:24 , פֶּן־תַּמְעִיטֵנִי “lest thou makest me (the people) few,” or “diminishest;” Eze 29:15.

(2) to make, or do anything a little, or in a slight degree (etwas in geringer Menge, in geringem Maaßbe thun). Num 11:32, הַמַּמְעִיט אָסַף עֲשָׂרָה חֳמָרִים “he who had (gathered) a little, had gathered ten homers.” Exo 16:17, 18 Exo 16:18 2Ki 4:3, “borrow empty vessels אַל־תַּמְעִיטִי sc. לִשְׁאֹל (borrow) not a few.” Specially to give few, Num 35:8 Exo 30:15. [Hence the following words.]
